cards.title: Having children makes you smarter
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than non-parents. The mental and emotional gymnastics of parenting appear to benefit cognitive health.

cards.title: Parents struggle supporting adult children not working
cards.body: Families face challenges when adult children cannot lead independent lives. Readers share experiences of caring for grown children facing employment or education difficulties.

cards.title: Banning phone use improved my parenting
cards.body: A psychotherapist reduced social media usage and saw dramatic improvements in mood and family relationships. Stricter phone limits helped create calmer interactions with her children.

cards.title: When your child's behavior triggers your past
cards.body: Parenting reactions often stem from unresolved childhood experiences rather than current behavior. Understanding these triggers helps parents respond more effectively to their children.

cards.title: Children with disabilities hurting siblings
cards.body: Children with disabilities or mental illness may behave aggressively toward siblings. Parents can take steps to help all children and maintain household safety.

cards.title: My children recovered from mental illness
cards.body: A parent's children suffered from psychiatric symptoms that resisted treatment for six years. A surprising diagnosis led to their rapid recovery and the parent's own healing.

cards.title: Adopted baby may have been trafficked
cards.body: A Singapore family's adoption turned troubling when they learned their baby may have been trafficked from Indonesia. The case highlights gaps in detecting child trafficking.

cards.title: Measles deaths surge in Bangladesh
cards.body: Over 120,000 suspected and confirmed measles cases reported in Bangladesh, overwhelming hospitals. The outbreak is tragic in a country that previously made major measles progress.
